lab_name: Ellipse Analytics (ISO 17025) via Clean Label Project
substances: heavy metals; bisphenols; phthalates; acrylamide; pesticides; glyphosate
notes: Only brand listed as Clean Label Project CERTIFIED in the Feb-2026 ISO-17025 study (independent testing for 100+ contaminants). Brand-level certification, not a per-lot COA.

product: Dry dog food category average (50 samples)
lab_name: Ellipse Analytics (ISO 17025)
substances: lead; arsenic; cadmium; mercury
result_value: lead avg 180.1; arsenic avg 184.6; cadmium avg 68.5; mercury avg 3.8; max lead 1576.5
notes: Independent ISO-17025 study: dry dog food averaged ~12.7x more lead and ~5.7x more arsenic than the human-consumables benchmark; highest single lead sample 1,576.5 ppb.

product: Fresh/frozen dog food category average (18 samples)
lab_name: Ellipse Analytics (ISO 17025)
substances: lead; arsenic; cadmium; mercury
result_value: lead avg 8.5; arsenic avg 13.9; cadmium avg 11.3; mercury avg 0.2
notes: Fresh/frozen segment had the LOWEST contaminants of any segment, below the human-consumables benchmark; dry food had ~21x more lead than fresh/frozen.

brand: Study: Kim et al. heavy metals in OTC dry dog foods
lab_name: Frontiers in Veterinary Science, 2018
substances: arsenic; cadmium; lead; mercury (ICP-MS)
result_value: red-meat median Pb 0.091; As 0.037; Cd 0.013; Hg 0.0012
notes: Frontiers in Vet Science 2018 (doi:10.3389/fvets.2018.00264). Fish diets higher As/Cd/Hg; red-meat diets higher Pb. Concluded foods generally safe for chronic consumption with occasional Pb outliers.

brand: Study: Koestel et al. BPA in canned dog food
lab_name: Science of the Total Environment, 2017
substances: bisphenol A (BPA)
result_value: serum BPA increased significantly in all dogs
notes: Sci Total Environ 2017;579:1804-1814. Both canned diets contained BPA incl. the 'BPA-free' one; serum BPA rose significantly after 2 weeks and correlated with microbiome changes.

brand: Study: Zhao et al. glyphosate in pet food (Cornell)
lab_name: Environmental Pollution, 2018
substances: glyphosate
notes: Environmental Pollution 2018 (Cornell). ALL 18 feeds contained glyphosate incl. one GMO-free certified; levels ~ <1% of human acceptable daily intake.

brand: Study: EWG PFAS in pet-food packaging
lab_name: Environmental Working Group, 2022
substances: PFAS (PFBA, PFPeA, PFHxA, PFHpA, 6:2 FTCA, 6:2 diPAP, PFPrA); total fluorine
result_value: Purina Cat Chow 244.7 total PFAS; Kibbles 'n Bits 14.3
notes: EWG Nov-2022 independent lab test of PACKAGING (total fluorine up to ~630 ppm); 7 PFAS detected. Purina Cat Chow Complete Chicken highest total PFAS (244.7 ppb). Migration into food not confirmed.

brand: Study/Recall: FDA Sportmix aflatoxin (Midwestern Pet Foods)
product: Sportmix, Pro Pac, Nunn Better lots
lab_name: U.S. FDA / Missouri Dept. of Agriculture, 2021
substances: aflatoxins
notes: FDA/Missouri Dept Ag 2021: samples up to 558 ppb aflatoxin vs 20 ppb adulteration threshold; >130 dog deaths. Government lab-confirmed.

lab_name: third-party accredited lab (AOAC; unnamed)
substances: arsenic; cadmium; lead; mercury
notes: Brand publishes a PUBLIC per-product heavy-metal results table with values + test dates (e.g. Simple Duck & Oatmeal As 0.30/Cd 0.22/Pb 0.08/Hg 0.02 mg/kg, all below NRC/FDA MTLs). Strong transparency but third-party lab unnamed and no downloadable independent COA -> graded brand_claim, values recorded.

lab_name: two third-party labs (AOAC; unnamed)
substances: arsenic; cadmium; lead; mercury
result_value: dog As 0.89; Cd 0.09; Pb 0.23; Hg 0.02 | cat As 1.36
notes: Brand white paper (May 2017) reports 3-yr third-party AOAC averages well below NRC/FDA MTLs (dog As 0.89/Cd 0.09/Pb 0.23/Hg 0.02; cat As 1.36 mg/kg). Aggregate, dated 2017 (consider stale), labs unnamed -> brand_claim.

lab_name: third-party QC lab (unnamed)
substances: Salmonella; E. coli; mycotoxins
notes: Real lot-code traceability tool: scan/enter the bag lot code to trace ingredients and DOWNLOAD per-run QC lab results (salmonella/E.coli/mycotoxin clearance). No specific lot COA captured here; verify with a real product lot.

lab_name: independent lab (unnamed)
substances: Salmonella; E. coli
notes: Real Lot ID lookup with a 'Download Report' COA per package (pathogen testing). No specific lot captured; verify with a real lot number.

lab_name: ISO 17025 food labs (unnamed)
substances: aflatoxin; DON; fumonisin; ochratoxin; zearalenone; Salmonella; STEC E. coli
notes: Enter 10-digit UPC + 7-digit lot; a Registered Vet Tech sends batch test results (mycotoxins/pathogens) from ISO-17025 labs. Batch-specific but delivered on request, not auto-published.
